diagrams.title: Input Output Devices Classification
diagrams.description: A hierarchical tree diagram showing the classification of input and output devices into their categories and subcategories, helping students understand the taxonomy of peripheral devices.
elements: Two main branches: Input Devices and Output Devices
Input sub-branches: Pointing (mouse, touchpad), Text Entry (keyboard), Image Capture (scanner, digital camera), Audio (microphone)
Output sub-branches: Visual (monitor, projector), Print (printer, plotter), Audio (speakers, headphones)
Icons or symbols for each device category
diagrams.purpose: Teaches students the organizational structure and categories of input and output devices, establishing foundational knowledge for computer graphics hardware

diagrams.title: Data Flow: Input to Output Process
diagrams.description: A flow diagram showing how data travels from input devices through the computer's processing unit and out through output devices, illustrating the input-processing-output cycle.
elements: Arrows showing data flow direction with labels (raw data, processed data, output)
diagrams.purpose: Explains the fundamental data processing cycle in computer graphics, showing how input devices capture data that gets processed and displayed as output

diagrams.title: Common Input Output Devices in Computer Graphics
diagrams.description: A visual reference panel showing commonly used input and output devices specifically relevant to computer graphics work, with brief labels.
elements: Input section: Keyboard, Mouse, Graphics Tablet, Scanner, Digital Camera, Microphone
Output section: CRT/LCD Monitor, Inkjet/Laser Printer, Plotter, Speakers, Projector
Color-coded borders distinguishing input (blue) from output (green)
Simple silhouette icons for each device
diagrams.purpose: Provides visual familiarity with the specific hardware used in computer graphics workflows, helping students recognize and differentiate devices
